summary: Blazing Lazy Lightning > Supplication and soaring Terrapin Station
blurb: The show erupts with a blistering Lazy Lightning > Supplication pair and a soaring Terrapin Station in the second set. Jerry’s guitar is on fire while Keith and Donna close out their final performance with the band. Recording quality varies, but the soundboard capture delivers clear detail of the high‑energy moments.
review: February 17, 1979 at the Oakland Coliseum captured one of the Dead’s most charged evenings as they wound down the Godchaux era. The first set builds momentum from a rousing “Greatest Story Ever Told” into an electric “Lazy Lightning > Supplication” stretch where Jerry Garcia is described as “on fire,” launching his solos into stratospheric highs. The second set continues the ascent with a powerful “Terrapin Station,” followed by a seamless flow through "Playing In The Band" into the classic “The Wheel > Shakedown Street” sequence, each transition praised for its tightness. Highlight moments are tempered by a few rough edges – Donna’s vocal on “From The Heart Of Me” wanders off‑key and “Passenger” suffers noticeable flubs – but these slips don’t eclipse the overall brilliance. Historically significant as Keith and Donna’s final appearance with the band, the night is preserved best on the clear soundboard source that showcases both the raw energy and the poignant farewell.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Six out of ten recordings are marked positive, two mixed and one negative. Positive remarks consistently highlight the Lazy Lightning > Supplication sequence, Terrapin Station, and the historic final appearance of Keith & Donna, while criticism is limited to a few vocal flubs. The strong consensus of praise yields a solid +1.4 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard mix with good balance; captures the high‑energy peaks (Lazy Lightning > Supplication, Terrapin Station) without dropouts, making it the most reliable listening source.

key_highlights: Blazing Lazy Lightning > Supplication sequence with soaring Garcia solos
Terrapin Station performance shines in the second set
Historic final appearance of Keith and Donna Godchaux on keyboards/vocals
Seamless transitions through The Wheel into Shakedown Street

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers fearless, soaring solos—especially on Lazy Lightning, Terrapin Station and the Bertha solo—described as ‘on fire’ and high‑note brilliance.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid, steady bass foundation throughout; reviewers note his playing is reliable and improves over the course of the night.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals are uneven at times, with off‑key moments on Passenger and From The Heart Of Me, but otherwise blend into the band’s flow.
band_performance.Keith: Marks his final show with the Dead; his piano contribution is noted as significant though some felt it was a bit listless early in the set.
